message:
feat(stdlib): port bsearch_r reentrant binary search (bd-eabvx)

Adds NetBSD's `void *bsearch_r(const void *key, const void *base,
size_t nmemb, size_t size, int (*compar)(const void *, const void
*, void *), void *thunk)` — the reentrant companion to POSIX
`bsearch`. Same algorithm but the comparator receives an extra
`void *thunk` argument so callers can carry context (case-
insensitive flag, sort-key index, etc.) without resorting to a
global.

Returns a pointer to the matching element on success, NULL on
no-match / NULL inputs / zero size or count / NULL comparator.

Implementation: thin abi shim implementing the standard
divide-and-conquer search via `low + (high - low) / 2` midpoint
arithmetic. The comparator is invoked with `(key, mid_elem,
thunk)`, matching NetBSD's argument order.

Tests: 6 abi integration tests covering: existing-element found,
missing-element returns NULL, leftmost+rightmost match, thunk
counter passes through unchanged on each comparator invocation,
NULL key/base/compar return NULL, and zero size or count return
NULL.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:

 {"id":"bd-e2ct","title":"syscall: close_range invalid flags on reversed bounds should still return EINVAL","description":"Host glibc returns EINVAL for close_range(5, 4, 0x80000000). Add ABI regression coverage so bad flags plus reversed bounds stay in the exact EINVAL bucket.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:32:32.686596433Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-22T00:43:27.336651943Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T00:43:27.334571755Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-e3y8","title":"wordexp parity: quote-aware badchar and command-substitution semantics","description":"Background:
- `wordexp` is marked Implemented, but current parsing is too naive about quoting/escaping context.
- Host glibc accepts quoted and escaped metacharacters literally and only rejects true unquoted shell separators.

Goal:
- Bring the native `wordexp` implementation into parity with host glibc for a bounded shell-lexing slice.

Deliverables:
1) Quote-aware/escape-aware bad-character validation for `wordexp`.
2) Quote-aware command-substitution detection so `WRDE_NOCMD` only rejects real command substitution.
3) Host-parity ABI tests for quoted semicolons, escaped separators, quoted command substitution literals, and bare bad-char cases.

Verification:
- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test unistd_abi_test wordexp_ -- --nocapture
- rch exec -- cargo check -p frankenlibc-abi --all-targets
- rch exec -- cargo clippy -p frankenlibc-abi --all-targets -- -D warnings","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T18:01:59.732205136Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T18:09:46.852185122Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T18:09:46.850227737Z","close_reason":"Match glibc wordexp quote/escape badchar and WRDE_NOCMD semantics with host-parity tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-e4h4","title":"resolv: extend async-DNS ABI tests with explicit GNU mode constants and badflags expectations","description":"The current unistd_abi async-DNS tests exercise degenerate success cases but not GNU mode semantics. Add explicit GAI_WAIT/GAI_NOWAIT/badflags constants in the ABI test layer and verify errno/output contracts for both valid and invalid mode combinations.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T06:39:25.697508593Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T18:14:17.906979579Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T18:14:17.904743048Z","close_reason":"Extended async-DNS ABI tests with explicit GNU mode and badflags constants and verified the invalid-mode errno contract against host libc.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-eabvx","title":"PORT: bsearch_r (NetBSD reentrant bsearch with thunk)","description":"Port the NetBSD `void *bsearch_r(const void *key, const void *base, size_t nmemb, size_t size, int (*compar)(const void *, const void *, void *), void *thunk)` reentrant binary search. Same as POSIX bsearch but the comparator receives an extra `void *thunk` parameter so callers can carry context (e.g. case-insensitive flag, sort key index) without a global. Implementation: thin abi shim implementing standard binary search via the existing memory layout helpers; compar invoked with (key, mid_elem, thunk). NULL key/base/compar returns NULL; size==0 or nmemb==0 returns NULL. Tests cover found / not-found, leftmost/rightmost positions, NULL guards, thunk passes through unchanged.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T11:58:39.348039681Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T11:58:39.348039681Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-edxwi","title":"syscall: renameat2 invalid flags should override null oldpath EFAULT with EINVAL","description":"Host glibc returns EINVAL for renameat2(AT_FDCWD, NULL, AT_FDCWD, ".", 0x80000000), so invalid flags dominate the null-source-path fault. Add ABI regression coverage for that precedence.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:29:10.460768794Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-22T04:12:15.819719390Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T04:12:15.817614396Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-ef08","title":"Conformance: direct execute memcpy_strict fixture cases","description":"Add direct frankenlibc_fixture_exec coverage for memcpy_strict.json cases in the harness alongside existing isolated harness coverage.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SilverHarbor","created_at":"2026-04-18T10:31:36.123405812Z","created_by":"SilverHarbor","updated_at":"2026-04-18T10:36:11.556412234Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T10:36:11.554552074Z","close_reason":"Added direct execute_fixture_case coverage for memcpy_strict fixtures and verified with rch test/check/clippy.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","string","testing"]}
 {"id":"bd-ef2","title":"I/O migration wave: route core unistd/io ABI entrypoints through raw veneer","description":"Critique mapping: #2.

Deliverables:
- Migrate open/close/read/write/pread64/pwrite64/lseek/fcntl subset used by smoke suite.
- Preserve strict mode ABI semantics and hardened mode evidence emission.

Acceptance:
- LD_PRELOAD smoke suite passes with migration enabled.
- Compatibility fixtures show no semantic drift on return values/errno.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCove","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:09.198369909Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T16:20:06.228805Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T16:20:06.228805Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","io","syscall"]}

 {"id":"bd-geoa","title":"resolv: getaddrinfo_a zero-item and null-request-list degenerate calls should succeed","description":"Host glibc returns success with errno unchanged for synchronous-degenerate getaddrinfo_a calls such as nitems=0 (even with a NULL list) and positive-length request arrays whose entries are all NULL. FrankenLibC currently returns EAI_SYSTEM/ENOSYS. Restore those no-op success semantics in unistd_abi.rs and lock them with focused ABI tests.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T06:27:52.529653096Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T06:30:33.157717821Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T06:30:33.155545909Z","close_reason":"Restored host-parity getaddrinfo_a success semantics for zero-item and all-NULL synchronous-degenerate request sets, with focused ABI regression coverage verified locally and via rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-gh8m4","title":"syscall: sched priority bounds for SCHED_DEADLINE should be zero exactly","description":"Host glibc returns min=0 and max=0 for sched_get_priority_{min,max}(SCHED_DEADLINE). Add focused ABI regression coverage so the Linux deadline-policy bounds contract is frozen exactly.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:33:47.405198256Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-22T04:42:33.365913964Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T04:42:33.363277112Z","close_reason":"Added sched_priority_bounds_sched_deadline_are_zero asserting min=max=0 host-parity for SCHED_DEADLINE (policy 6).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-ghm72","title":"PORT: bindresvport6 (glibc IPv6 reserved-port bind helper)","description":"Port the glibc IPv6 reserved-port bind helper `int bindresvport6(int sd, struct sockaddr_in6 *sin6)`. Companion to the existing IPv4 bindresvport — binds the socket to a privileged IPv6 port (512..1023) by trying ports in a process-id-rotated order until one binds successfully. If `sin6` is NULL, an in6addr_any sockaddr_in6 is synthesized. Returns 0 on success, -1 on failure with errno set (EADDRINUSE if no reserved port is available). Implementation: mirror the existing bindresvport flow in rpc_abi but use sockaddr_in6 + AF_INET6. Tests: bind to a fresh socket succeeds and reports the port within the reserved range; supplying a NULL sockaddr works (function uses internal storage); EADDRINUSE-vs-other-error path is exercised when bind hits a real failure (e.g. closed-socket EBADF).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T11:51:12.585026069Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T11:51:12.585026069Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-ghm72","title":"PORT: bindresvport6 (glibc IPv6 reserved-port bind helper)","description":"Port the glibc IPv6 reserved-port bind helper `int bindresvport6(int sd, struct sockaddr_in6 *sin6)`. Companion to the existing IPv4 bindresvport — binds the socket to a privileged IPv6 port (512..1023) by trying ports in a process-id-rotated order until one binds successfully. If `sin6` is NULL, an in6addr_any sockaddr_in6 is synthesized. Returns 0 on success, -1 on failure with errno set (EADDRINUSE if no reserved port is available). Implementation: mirror the existing bindresvport flow in rpc_abi but use sockaddr_in6 + AF_INET6. Tests: bind to a fresh socket succeeds and reports the port within the reserved range; supplying a NULL sockaddr works (function uses internal storage); EADDRINUSE-vs-other-error path is exercised when bind hits a real failure (e.g. closed-socket EBADF).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T11:51:12.585026069Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T11:54:37.317641728Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T11:54:37.315614378Z","close_reason":"shipped: bindresvport6 IPv6 reserved-port bind mirroring bindresvport with AF_INET6 sockaddr_in6 + 3 abi tests, all green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-gii3","title":"raise(0) incorrectly rejects POSIX null signal","description":"Our frankenlibc_abi::signal_abi::raise() checks valid_signal(sig) which requires sig in 1..=64. POSIX specifies raise(0) must succeed as the 'null signal' (no-op sending, checks tgkill permissions only). Host glibc's raise(0) does tgkill(pid,tid,0) which succeeds. Impacts gsignal(0) via raise(). Fix: add explicit null-signal handling to signal_abi::raise() before valid_signal check, calling tgkill(pid,tid,0) directly. Discovered while expanding signal_ops conformance fixtures (bd-9n4k).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T17:45:39.193198411Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T17:56:13.339368276Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T17:56:13.337394740Z","close_reason":"Implemented POSIX null-signal raise/gsignal semantics","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-gijn2","title":"REVIEW: [HIGH] inet_abi entry points walk unbounded user-supplied pointers (bd-z4k96 class)","description":"FOUND + FIXED via review round 5 (commit 0c0b59f1).

Four ABI entry points (inet_pton, inet_aton, inet_addr, if_nametoindex) dereferenced caller-supplied *const c_char via CStr::from_ptr with no length bound. A non-NUL-terminated argument walked arbitrary process memory until hitting an accidental zero byte — same info-leak / segfault class as bd-z4k96 (locale), cab4670a (iconv), 6f1e3036 (dlfcn).

Reachability: every program that parses an IP address from caller-controlled text (config readers, network-protocol parsers, getaddrinfo fallbacks) hits inet_pton/inet_aton/inet_addr; if_nametoindex is reached by every routing/iface-options call.

Fix: add read_bounded_cstr (mirrors locale/iconv/dlfcn copies) and replace each unbounded CStr::from_ptr; on None return the function's standard invalid-input sentinel.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod-frankenlibc","created_at":"2026-04-24T07:45:00Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-24T07:45:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T07:45:00Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commits 0c0b59f1 / 1d68447a / fe3f1c06; 3120/3120 abi tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bd-z4k96-class","high","review","unbounded-cstr"]}
 {"id":"bd-gilq3","title":"[HIGH] bd15n2_fixture_gap_fill_artifacts_test fails: 'could not locate libfrankenlibc_abi.so'","description":"## Symptom

`cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test bd15n2_fixture_gap_fill_artifacts_test` fails:

```
thread 'fixture_gap_fill_gate_emits_valid_bd15n2_artifacts' panicked at
crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/bd15n2_fixture_gap_fill_artifacts_test.rs:42:5:
gate script failed:
stdout=
stderr=    Finished `release` profile [optimized] target(s) in 0.06s
FAIL: could not locate libfrankenlibc_abi.so
```

## Diagnosis

The test at line 42 invokes a gate script (presumably under scripts/) that needs the built `libfrankenlibc_abi.so` cdylib. The script builds a release profile of frankenlibc-abi (that step succeeds in the above log), then fails to *find* the resulting .so at the path it expects. The stderr only shows the cargo 'Finished release' line, no locator stderr from the script — the script likely greps `target/release` for the .so but we're running under `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cc` which the script doesn't respect.

## Fix direction

1. Read crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/bd15n2_fixture_gap_fill_artifacts_test.rs:42 and trace which gate script it spawns.
2. That script likely hardcodes `target/release/libfrankenlibc_abi.so`; change it to honour `CARGO_TARGET_DIR` (or at least look at `/data/tmp/cargo-target/release/libfrankenlibc_abi.so` when set) or fall back to `cargo metadata --format-version=1 | jq -r .target_directory`/`release/libfrankenlibc_abi.so`.

## Blast radius

Fails even in-isolation — test is always broken under a non-default `CARGO_TARGET_DIR`. Blocks the bd15n2 fixture-gap-fill gate from being exercised in any CI environment that redirects target_dir (rch, sccache, CI caches, etc.).

## Verification

- `cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test bd15n2_fixture_gap_fill_artifacts_test` — reproduces reliably with `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cc`.
- Expected to pass under default `CARGO_TARGET_DIR` (unset → defaults to `target/`).
","notes":"Fixed by cc-libc in the same push.

Root cause: scripts/bd15n2_fixture_gap_fill_run.sh maintained a hardcoded LIB_CANDIDATES array of three paths (target/release, target/debug, /data/tmp/cargo-target/release) and never considered a caller-supplied CARGO_TARGET_DIR. Under rch (which redirects to /tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cc) or any other non-default target-dir setup, the script's rebuild step put the .so somewhere the candidate list never looked.

Fix: append "${CARGO_TARGET_DIR}/release/libfrankenlibc_abi.so" and "${CARGO_TARGET_DIR}/debug/libfrankenlibc_abi.so" to LIB_CANDIDATES when the env var is set, matching the pattern that scripts/e2e_suite.sh already uses correctly.

Extended the same fix to scripts/c_fixture_suite.sh and scripts/ld_preload_smoke.sh, which had the identical bug — those are called by sibling gates that would have tripped next.

Also improved the error message to print the searched-path list when the library can't be located, so future breakages are diagnosable at a glance.

Validation: rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cc cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test bd15n2_fixture_gap_fill_artifacts_test -> 1 passed, 0 failed, 28.96s (worker vmi1152480).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cc-libc","created_at":"2026-04-22T13:18:06.994113107Z","created_by":"cc-libc","updated_at":"2026-04-22T13:46:11.806847Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T13:46:11.806847Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cdylib","full-review","harness","test-infra"]}
